name: vue-debug-guides
description: Vue 3 debugging and error handling for runtime errors, warnings, async failures, and SSR/hydration issues. Use when diagnosing or fixing Vue issues.
capabilities: Diagnose reactivity issues like unexpected re-renders and ref value problems
Debug computed property bugs including array mutations and conditional dependencies
Fix watcher issues including async cleanup, flush timing, and deep comparison problems
Resolve component issues like missing refs, event handling, and prop/emit errors
Troubleshoot SSR hydration mismatches and cross-request state pollution
Identify template issues including v-for keys, v-if order, and scoped style problems
author: hyf0
securitySummary: All static scanner findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a documentation skill containing markdown files with Vue debugging guides. The scanner incorrectly flags markdown code blocks (backtick syntax), URLs in documentation, and frontmatter metadata as security issues. No actual security risks present. This skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Vue 3 applications can exhibit mysterious runtime errors, unexpected re-renders, and SSR hydration issues that are difficult to diagnose. This skill provides comprehensive debugging guides for identifying and fixing common Vue 3 pitfalls across reactivity, components, templates, and server-side rendering.
limitations: Does not provide interactive debugging tools or runtime inspectors
Covers Vue 3 only, not Vue 2 specific issues
Does not include code execution or automated fixing capabilities
Reference guides assume basic familiarity with Vue Composition API

useCases.title: Fix Reactivity Debugging
useCases.description: When Vue components exhibit unexpected re-renders or state fails to update, use the reactivity debugging guides to trace dependency changes and fix ref/reactive issues.

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers working with Nuxt or Vue SSR
useCases.title: Resolve SSR Hydration Errors
useCases.description: When server-side rendered Vue apps show hydration mismatch warnings or errors, consult the SSR guides to identify root causes and implement proper client-server alignment.

useCases.title: Debug Component Communication
useCases.description: When parent-child component communication breaks due to missing props, emit issues, or ref problems, use the component debugging guides to trace data flow and fix binding issues.

promptTemplates.title: Debug Basic Reactivity
promptTemplates.prompt: My Vue 3 ref is not updating in the template. I declared it with const count = ref(0) and use {{ count }} in template but changes don't show. How do I fix this?
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner developer encountering ref.value access issue

promptTemplates.title: Fix Computed Array Mutation
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm using a computed property to sort an array, but when I reverse it, the original data changes too. The computed seems to be mutating state. What went wrong?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer dealing with computed side effects

promptTemplates.title: Resolve Hydration Mismatch
promptTemplates.prompt: My Nuxt app shows hydration mismatch warnings. The HTML differs between server and client. The console shows dates rendered differently. How do I debug this?

promptTemplates.title: Debug Watcher Not Firing
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a deep: true watcher on a reactive object, but it reports the same old and new values. Also, my async watcher sometimes has stale data issues. What is happening?
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced developer with complex watcher patterns

bestPractices: Always use unique keys in v-for loops based on data IDs, never array indices, to maintain correct DOM state and animation behavior
Use watchEffect with flush: 'post' when you need to access DOM elements or template refs after updates complete
Declare all emits using defineEmits for proper TypeScript support and better component documentation
antiPatterns: Destructuring reactive objects with const { name } = reactive(obj) which breaks reactivity by losing the proxy reference
Using v-if with v-for on the same element, which causes precedence issues - use a wrapper element with v-for and v-if on different elements
Calling provide/inject outside of setup context or asynchronously, which causes values to be undefined or lost

faq.question: What is this skill best used for?
faq.answer: This skill provides debugging guides for Vue 3 applications. Use it when you encounter runtime errors, unexpected behavior, or warnings that you cannot explain. It covers reactivity, computed properties, watchers, components, templates, SSR, and more.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Vue 2?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses specifically on Vue 3 debugging. Many concepts differ between Vue 2 and Vue 3, especially around the Composition API, reactivity system, and lifecycle hooks.

faq.question: Can this skill fix my code automatically?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides documentation and guidance for identifying and understanding Vue 3 issues. It does not execute code or make automatic fixes. You apply the recommended patterns in your code.

faq.question: What topics does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers 140+ debugging scenarios across reactivity, computed properties, watchers, components, props, emits, templates, template refs, forms, events, lifecycle hooks, slots, provide/inject, composables, composition API, animations, TypeScript, async components, render functions, KeepAlive, transitions, teleport, suspense, and SSR.

faq.question: Do I need TypeScript to use these guides?
faq.answer: No, most guides work with plain JavaScript. TypeScript-specific guides exist for type-related issues, but they explain the underlying concepts that apply to both TS and JS projects.

faq.question: How is this different from Vue best practices?
faq.answer: Best practices guide you on how to write code correctly from the start. This debugging skill helps you identify and fix issues when something goes wrong. Use vue-best-practices for prevention and vue-debug-guides for diagnosis.
